title: cornus macrophylla var. stracheyi
intro: Cornus macrophylla var. stracheyi, also known as Himalayan Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayas. Its bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Cornus macrophylla var. stracheyi is traditionally prepared as a decoction or tincture, and its extracts are used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including diarrhea, dysentery, and skin conditions.
